steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare Argentina's decline in hours worked to other countries. Because the countries had different baseline hours in 1950, percent change is the correct metric for comparison, not absolute change in hours.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the decline in number of hours worked was greater in Argentina than it was in Ireland, the United Kingdom, or Mexico.: This compares absolute hour decreases, not percent changes. Argentina's absolute decline is not the largest, and using raw hours ignores different starting points. ✗
  • Choice B — while the number of hours worked rose in Argentina between 1950 and 2017, it declined in Ireland, the United Kingdom, and Mexico.: Hours worked in Argentina declined between 1950 and 2017 — this is factually wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — though the percent decrease in hours worked in Argentina was greater than that in Ireland and Mexico, it was less than that in the United Kingdom.: Argentina's percent change is -17%. Ireland's is -28% and the UK's is -24%, both larger than -17%, so Argentina's decline is not greater than Ireland's. ✗
  • Choice D — though the percent decrease in hours worked in Argentina was less than that in Ireland and the United Kingdom, it was greater than that in Mexico.: Argentina (-17%) is smaller in magnitude than Ireland (-28%) and the UK (-24%), but larger than Mexico (-3%). This is accurate on all counts. ✓
That matches Choice D — though the percent decrease in hours worked in Argentina was less than that in Ireland and the United Kingdom, it was greater than that in Mexico..
